subject: PLEASE READ - Clubhouse Reopening Today at Noon – Limited Access & Parking
html:
From: "Kristine Russo Diaz (Replies Not Accepted)"
Date: 2026-02-25
Time: 10-11-24
Subject: PLEASE READ - Clubhouse Reopening Today at Noon – Limited Access & Parking
Good morning Homeowners,
 
We are on track to reopen the clubhouse today at noon. Please note that entry will be through the front entrance under the portico only.
 
At this time, most of the clubhouse parking lot will remain closed as work is still underway on the Southside of the building. Parking is extremely limited, with only a few spaces available at the clubhouse and additional limited parking at the sports complex.
 
While we love having our wonderful homeowners enjoy the clubhouse, please be advised that the pool and hot tub areas remain closed, and this is still an active construction site. Certain areas may be hazardous, and although we have clearly marked safe entry points, conditions remain challenging.
 
For your safety, we strongly recommend that you only come to the clubhouse today if you have official business or absolutely need to be here. If your visit can be postponed, it would be best to wait until construction activity is further along.
 
Please also note that construction noise will be significant throughout the day.
 
Thank you for your patience, understanding, and cooperation as we continue to move this large project forward.
 
Sincerely,
Kristine Diaz
Community Association Manager
Avalon Estates

subject: Pool Closure Update & Neighboring Community Pool Access
html:
From: "Kristine Russo Diaz (Replies Not Accepted)"
Date: 2026-03-09
Time: 16-25-26
Subject: Pool Closure Update & Neighboring Community Pool Access
Dear Homeowners,
 
While our pool remains closed, we are still able to use the pool facilities at Coral Lakes and Valencia Falls.
 
In order to access the pool at Coral Lakes, your name must be added to their approved guest list. If you would like to go and have not yet been added, please call or email me with your name and I will forward it to their manager.
 
We are also still able to use the pool at Valencia Falls; however, there are a few updates for this week:
 
Wednesday: The Valencia Falls clubhouse water will be shut off at 2:00 PM for repairs.
Friday: Their pool will be closed until 2:00 PM for leak detection.
 
Because of these closures, you may want to plan on using Coral Lakes on Wednesday afternoon and Friday morning.
 
I am also waiting to hear back from our other neighboring communities, who were gracious enough to allow us to use their pools, to confirm whether we will be able to continue accessing their facilities.
 
Thank you for your patience and understanding while we work through the repairs to our roof. I will continue to keep you updated as we receive additional information.
 
Sincerely,
Kristine Diaz
Community Association Manager
Avalon Estates

subject: Yoga is Coming to Avalon the first week of February
html:
From: "Kristine Russo Diaz (Replies Not Accepted)"
Date: 2026-01-30
Time: 09-42-46
Subject: Yoga is Coming to Avalon the first week of February
Yoga is Coming to Avalon
the first week of February!!!
 
CLASSES START ON MONDAY, FEBRUARY 2nd
in the BALLROOM.
PLEASE SIGN-UP ON SHEET OUTSIDE CARD ROOM FOR FEBRUARY.
 
 
Fee – Monthly fee is $35 per MONTH per class, or $60 for both per MONTH. Payment is due at the last session of each month. Drop-in fee of $10 is based on availability. Exact cash or check made out to Jen’s Fusion Fitness please. Payment for the month will be due at the first session for February (thereafter due by the last week of each month.)
 
Chair/Beginners’ Yoga – A gentle flow of seated and standing exercises and poses to help flexibility, mobility, balance and stability. Mondays, 4:15-5pm. Light shoes, socks, or bare feet. Meet in the ballroom. No prior experience necessary.
 
Yoga Flow Mat – Improve flexibility, mobility, strength, and stability through a flow of standing, seated, and lying poses focusing on posture & alignment while centering yourself with breath-work, mindfulness, and meditation. Wednesdays, 4:15-5pm. Meet in the ballroom. Yoga mat needed, small towel recommended, bare feet or yoga socks. 
 
Instructor – Jennifer Penfield, Exercise Physiologist and Integrated Nutrition Health Coach. 
 
Registration – Sign-up required at the last session of each month for your place in class for the following month. For the first class in February, there is a sign-up sheet on the bulletin board outside the Card Room. Payment for the month will be due at the first session.
 
Questions? Text Jennifer at 561-267-9506 if you have questions.

subject: IMPORTANT: Clubhouse Access Changes – Safety Notice (Must Follow Posted Signs)
html:
From: "Kristine Russo Diaz (Replies Not Accepted)"
Date: 2026-03-06
Time: 15-57-07
Subject: IMPORTANT: Clubhouse Access Changes – Safety Notice (Must Follow Posted Signs)
Dear Homeowners,
 
This is an important safety notice regarding the clubhouse next week. Please read carefully and follow all posted signs.
 
Beginning Monday through Wednesday of next week, the main entrance to the clubhouse will be completely closed due to the roof tile deliveries. For your safety AND the safety of the crews working on-site, this area will be fully restricted.
 
Important:
  • You will NOT be able to drive up to the front entrance.
  • You may NOT walk up or enter through the front doors.
  • All access to the clubhouse must be through the ballroom side entrance (fitness center side).
Please obey all posted signs, cones, and barriers around the clubhouse during this time. These measures are in place strictly for safety, and we ask that everyone respect them.
 
Additionally, Sunday evening we will be blocking off additional parking spaces in the clubhouse parking lot in preparation for the tile delivery.
 
Parking will be limited near the clubhouse Monday–Wednesday.

We truly appreciate your patience, cooperation, and understanding as we work through this important improvement project.
 
Thank you for helping us keep the community safe.
 
Sincerely,
Kristine Diaz
Community Association Manager
Avalon Estates

subject: Roof Project Timeline Update – Start Date Adjustment
html:
From: "Kristine Russo Diaz (Replies Not Accepted)"
Date: 2026-01-28
Time: 15-10-38
Subject: Roof Project Timeline Update – Start Date Adjustment
Dear Residents,
 
As we indicated in our earlier communication, all project dates remain tentative and are dependent on permit approval. With that in mind—and based on the feedback we have received from many of you—we want you to know that we hear you.
 
Several residents shared concerns regarding the originally anticipated start date falling during a week when family and guests will be visiting. In response and given that permit approval may still shift our timeline, the Board has decided to adjust the tentative project start date to February 23rd.
 
This change reflects both the uncertainty surrounding permit issuance and our commitment to being mindful of your schedules and comfort during a busy holiday week.
 
We truly appreciate your patience, your communication, and your trust as we work through this process. We will continue to provide updates as soon as new information becomes available.
 
Sincerely, 
Kristine Diaz
Community Association Manager
Avalon Estates

subject: Avalon Roof Replacement Project Update 3.2.26
html:
From: "Kristine Russo Diaz (Replies Not Accepted)"
Date: 2026-03-02
Time: 12-20-18
Subject: Avalon Roof Replacement Project Update 3.2.26
It was a very busy week at the Avalon Estates buildings complex. The clubhouse, guardhouse and tennis buildings were stripped of the existing tile and significant repairs to the plywood substrate and the fascia of all three buildings were made. The protective underlayment layers have been put down and all the metal, drip edges, vents and solar fans have been installed. 
 
This coming week the process will continue with stucco work and painting on all 3 buildings. In addition our solar vendor, Urban Solar, will begin installing more than 600 brackets that will eventually hold the solar panels. Tile deliveries are projected for the week beginning March 8th.
 
One significant area of concern was discovered during the roof tear down. The roof over the pool was found to have structural damage due to water intrusion and for the safety of the Evans team work on that section was halted while we evaluate the severity of the damage. We have been working with our engineer from Falcon and have met with two different contractors so far to evaluate a path forward. 
 
Until this is resolved this portion of the roof will remain unfinished. The safety of our residents and our vendors will drive any decisions in this area. Despite this temporary setback as of this update the project is slightly ahead of schedule. 
 
 
The entire project management team sincerely appreciates your patience and understanding and will continue to provide updates as needed.
 
Thank you,
Ric Gonzalez

subject: Use of Other Communities' Pools while our pool is closed
html:
From: "Kristine Russo Diaz (Replies Not Accepted)"
Date: 2026-02-19
Time: 12-33-53
Subject: Use of Other Communities' Pools while our pool is closed
Dear residents,
 
As you know, replacement of our clubhouse roof necessitates closure of our pool, which is expected to be for two weeks from February 23 to March 7, 2026.  
 
Three neighboring communities have graciously agreed to let our residents use their pools while ours is closed:  Coral Lakes, Valencia Falls, and Valencia Lakes.   Please see details below.  Although our pool closure may be an inconvenience during this project, this is a wonderful opportunity to experience other community pools.   We're always looking for ideas to improve our community --  take note of the pool features you would like to see here at Avalon Estates and pass along any suggestions to the business office.  This will give us a great head start on plans for redoing our pool in a few years!
 
Coral Lakes:  
Directions - Turn left on Flavor Pict, turn left on El Clair Ranch Rd, then left into main entrance.  At the gatehouse, stop at the speaker box and announce that you are an Avalon Estates resident to use the pool.  Enter the Clubhouse main entrance and sign in at the front desk.  The attendant there will direct you to the pool.
 
Pool Location and Hours:  Please let the Avalon Estates business office know this week if you plan to use the Coral Lakes pool.  We will provide them with a list of prospective attendees.  The pool is located behind the clubhouse and is open to Avalon Estates residents from Noon to Sunset.  There are locker rooms off of the lobby, down one floor.  Either take the stairs and/or the elevator down to  -1.  Once off the elevator, walk straight and go thru the double doors at the end of the hallway, where the locker rooms will be on your left.  First is the Men’s Locker Room and further down is the Women’s Locker Room.  The access to both the indoor and/or outdoor pool is located right there.
 
Cafe:  You are welcome to purchase items from the cafe located up the ramp from the pool deck.  The Cafe accepts cash and credit cards and is open 8:30 am to 3:00 pm.  One can also go directly to the Café from the Lobby of the Clubhouse, by turning right and going straight down the hall to the end, where the CL Café is located.
 
Theatre:  Avalon Estates Residents are welcome to attend the Coral Lakes Shows.  Box Office is located in the lobby.  Let “Lisa” know that you are a resident from Avalon Estates and interested in purchasing tickets to a show.  Checks and credit cards are accepted.  No cash, though.  Thank you!
 

Valencia Falls:
Directions - Valencia Falls is located on Hagen Ranch Road.  Turn right from our entrance and at the second stop light, turn left into Valencia Falls.  It is directly across from Polo Trace.  Tell the guard you are from Avalon Estates to use the pool.  
 
Pool Location and Hours:  The Clubhouse and pool are directly across the lake from the guardhouse.  Turn right and go halfway around the circle.  Turn left into the Club driveway.  The walkway to the pool is on the left side of the building.   The pool is open from sunrise to sunset.
 
Cafe:  You are welcome to use the cafe, which accepts credit cards and cash.
 
When they come to the gate they just need to mention that they’re from Avalon Estates and coming to use the pool.
Our pool will be closed on 2/27 due to repairs.
Also Monday - Thursday we have water aerobics classes from 10:30-11:30 so if they come during that time they will just need to stay at the other end of the pool.
 
Valencia Lakes:
Directions - Valencia Lakes is located on Hagen Ranch Road.  Turn left from our entrance and at the first stoplight, turn left into the Valencia Lakes entrance.  Tell the guard you are from Avalon Estates to use the pool.  After the guard house, turn either left or right at the stop sign and drive halfway around the circle.  Turn into the Excelsior Club, which is the clubhouse and pool location.  The pool is to the right of the building.  No fob is necessary to enter the pool area.
 
Pool Location and Hours:  Look for the Excelsior Club sign, which is where the clubhouse and pool are located.  The pool is to the right of the building.   Please park on the pavers on the Blvd outside of the Excelsior Club sign.  The Lap pool is available from noon to sunset.  The Resort pool is available from 7 am to sunset.  The Valencia Lakes Pool rules, which are very similar to ours, are copied below for your review.

subject: IMPORTANT: Clubhouse, Fitness Center, Pool & Parking Closures – Roof Project Underway
html:
From: "Kristine Russo Diaz (Replies Not Accepted)"
Date: 2026-02-18
Time: 09-11-06
Subject: IMPORTANT: Clubhouse, Fitness Center, Pool & Parking Closures – Roof Project Underway
Dear Homeowners,
 
We are pleased to let you know that the roof replacement project for the Clubhouse, Guardhouse, and Tennis Building is now underway. While this is an important improvement for our community, it will require several temporary closures for safety reasons.
 
Please read the information below carefully and mark these dates on your calendar.
 
IMPORTANT DATES AND CLOSURES
 
Friday, February 20th
The main entrance to the clubhouse will be CLOSED until 12:30 PM for rooftop supply deliveries.
During this time, clubhouse access will be through the Ballroom entrance only until the main/front entrance is reopened.
Portions of the clubhouse parking lot will be CLOSED and unavailable for resident parking on this day.

Beginning Monday, February 23rd – Clubhouse, Pool & Spa Closures
On Monday, February 23rd, the ENTIRE clubhouse, fitness center and clubhouse parking lot will be CLOSED for the day. No homeowners may enter the clubhouse on this date.
 
Also beginning Monday, February 23rd, the swimming pool, spa, and pool deck area will be CLOSED for approximately two (2) weeks.
 
The exact reopening date will be determined based on construction progress and weather. If work is completed sooner, we will reopen earlier. If delays occur due to weather or unforeseen circumstances, the closure may need to be extended. Following this email, you will receive another email communication with information from neighboring communities who have graciously offered to allow our residents to use their pool during this closure, so please keep an eye out for that message.
 
Tuesday, February 24th through Saturday, February 28th – Limited Clubhouse Access
The clubhouse will be OPEN DAILY from 12:00 PM (noon) until 11:00 PM.

Morning access will not be available during this period.
The main/front entrance may be closed at times, with access through the Ballroom entrance only. This will be determined as the project progresses. We will communicate any changes by email, and posted signs and caution tape will direct residents to the appropriate entrances and restricted areas.
Some parking areas will be closed. Please follow posted signage.
 
SAFETY REMINDERS
Please follow all posted parking restrictions.
Do NOT move any cones, barriers, or caution tape.
Do NOT walk under any closed-off or restricted areas.
These safety measures are in place because construction materials or tools could fall and cause serious injury or damage.
 
We understand that these temporary closures may be inconvenient, especially for residents who regularly use the clubhouse or pool. We sincerely apologize for any disruption to your normal schedules, including fitness, gaming and social activities. Our goal is to complete this project as quickly and safely as possible while keeping Avalon Estates beautiful and well-maintained.
 
Additional closures may be necessary as the project progresses. We will continue to keep you informed by email and with notices posted inside the clubhouse. Please feel free to share this information with your neighbors and friends who may not check email regularly.
 
Thank you for your patience and cooperation as we work to improve our community.
 
Sincerely,
Kristine Diaz
Community Association Manager
Avalon Estates

subject: Annual Meeting (Reconvened Session) – Thursday, February 26 at 6:30 PM
html:
From: "Kristine Russo Diaz (Replies Not Accepted)"
Date: 2026-02-17
Time: 13-06-50
Subject: Annual Meeting (Reconvened Session) – Thursday, February 26 at 6:30 PM
Dear Homeowners,
 
This is a reminder that the Annual Meeting of the Members will be reconvened in the Ballroom on Thursday, February 26, 2026, at 6:30 PM.
 
This reconvened meeting is a continuation of the Annual Meeting that was recessed. Proxies and ballots previously submitted will remain valid.
 
The purpose of this reconvened meeting is solely to complete the membership vote on the proposed amendment to Article X, Section F, which would revise the approval requirement for future amendments to require a two-thirds (2/3) vote of the members present at a meeting, either in person, by absentee ballot, or by proxy, at which a quorum is obtained.
 
Once the results of this vote are established, the Annual Meeting will be concluded, and the regular monthly meeting of the Board of Directors will begin.
 
We appreciate your participation and encourage all members to attend. If you have not yet voted, you may do so now and up until the day of the meeting, at which time balloting will be closed as announced.
 
Sincerely,
Kristine Diaz
Community Association Manager
Avalon Estates

Subject: Yoga is Coming - Pending Board Approval
Yoga is Coming to Avalon
in February!!!
 
PLEASE SIGN-UP ON SHEET OUTSIDE CARD ROOM FOR FEBRUARY
 
 
Chair/Beginners’ Yoga – A gentle flow of seated and standing exercises and poses to help flexibility, mobility, balance and stability. Mondays, 4:15-5pm. Light shoes, socks, or bare feet. No prior experience necessary.
 
Yoga Flow Mat – Improve flexibility, mobility, strength, and stability through a flow of standing, seated, and lying poses focusing on posture & alignment while centering yourself with breath-work, mindfulness, and meditation. Wednesdays, 4:15-5pm. Yoga mat needed, small towel recommended, bare feet or yoga socks. 
 
Instructor – Jennifer Penfield, Exercise Physiologist and Integrated Nutrition Health Coach. 
 
Registration – Sign-up required at the last session of each month for your place in class for the following month. For the first class in February, there is a sign-up sheet on the bulletin board outside the Card Room. Payment will be due at the first session.
 
Fee – Monthly fee is $35 per class, or $60 for both. Payment is due at the last session of each month. Drop-in fee of $10 is based on availability.
Exact cash or check please. 
 
Questions? Text Jennifer at 561-267-9506 if you have questions. 
 
*** Classes to begin first week of February, pending Board Approval on January 22nd, 2026.

Subject: Roof / Solar Project Updates
 
Fellow residents, as Project Coordinator for various projects in our community, I wanted to give you some idea on what to expect over the next several weeks as we begin the roof replacement and solar panel installation project. 
 
Please keep in mind there are a lot of moving parts and the dates you see below are the best estimates we currently have. Factors such as weather, city and county permits and inspections, material delays and so on can impact the timeline. These dates were discussed and developed with the various project managers assigned to the project based on their experience. These dates only reflect the clubhouse project and do not take into account the roof replacements for the Gatehouse and the Tennis building. subject: Notice Regarding TV Channel Message
html:
From: "Kristine Russo Diaz (Replies Not Accepted)"
Date: 2026-04-02
Time: 10-31-17
Subject: Notice Regarding TV Channel Message
Dear Homeowners, 
 
You may notice a message appearing on some of your TV channels stating that certain channels are unavailable and referencing an expired contract with EW Scripps Company.
A copy of the message is included below for your reference:
 
Please be advised that this message is not related to Avalon Estates or the Association’s contract. The notice refers to a separate agreement between Xfinity and EW Scripps Company. Unfortunately, this matter is outside of the Association’s control, and we do not have the ability to influence or resolve it.
 
We understand this may be frustrating and appreciate your patience while Xfinity works toward a resolution.
 
Thank you for your understanding.
 
Sincerely,
Kristine Diaz
Community Association Manager
Avalon Estates 


Some of your channels are unavailable
 
Our contract with E.W. Scripps Company has expired. Scripps is a local broadcasting group that owns many TV stations across the country. Despite our best efforts, we have not been able to reach a new agreement. This means that some of your local channels and On Demand content are currently unavailable with Xfinity. To see which channels are affected in your area, visit xfinity.com/facts.
 
We know unexpected changes to your channel lineup can be frustrating. Scripps has refused our reasonable offers and is making demands that would significantly increase what you pay for TV service. Thanks for your patience as we work to keep your TV package affordable. If we reach a new agreement and the channels are restored to your lineup, we’ll email you again.
 
Great content remains available through Xfinity:                                                  
To find similar content on X1, say “Explore my options” into your Xfinity Voice Remote.
Many local news stations remain available on X1 – just say “Free News” into your Xfinity Voice Remote – or visit your local station’s website to see if they stream their news broadcasts online for free.

Xfinity still offers all your favorite players, teams, and sports in one place. Say “Sports” into your Xfinity Voice Remote to easily find them all.
Thanks for being with Xfinity.

subject: Avalon Roof Replacement Update from Ric Gonzalez
html:
From: "Kristine Russo Diaz (Replies Not Accepted)"
Date: 2026-04-02
Time: 14-13-28
Subject: Avalon Roof Replacement Update from Ric Gonzalez
Dear Homeowners,
 
We would like to provide you with an update on the roof replacement, solar installation, and pool roof repair projects.
 
Roof Replacement
Weather permitting the roof replacement should be completed, minus the pool roof by Monday the 6th. Gutter installation should start on the 13th of April. Once the pool roof repair is complete Evans will return to finish the tile on that portion of the roof.
 
Solar Installation
Underground boring to connect the main transformer to the clubhouse for the solar system is underway. Panel installation and final wiring will start next week. Power to the clubhouse will need to be shut down for several hours to facilitate the project. This is currently scheduled for April 8th.Watch for communications from our Community Manager.
 
Pool Roof Repair
Temporary shoring of the roof is in place. We expect the final shoring to be completed late next week. Drawings for the repair of the structural damage are being created. Once we have these we will reach out for quotes for the actual repairs. 
 
It appears that we may have to drain the pool in order to accommodate the repairs. As we get more information we will update you, however in the meantime the pool and pool deck will remain closed.
 
Sincerely, 
Ric Gonzalez
Project Coordinator

subject: Clubhouse Project Update: Progress and Next Steps
html:
From: "Kristine Russo Diaz (Replies Not Accepted)"
Date: 2026-04-13
Time: 15-29-53
Subject: Clubhouse Project Update: Progress and Next Steps
Dear Homeowners,
 
Here is the latest update on current activities and the outlook for ongoing projects at the clubhouse:
 
Roof Tile Replacement
Evans is expected to complete the roof installation by the end of the day Monday. Gutter installation is also scheduled to begin on Monday. Once the roof is complete, vents will be painted to match.
 
Photovoltaic (Solar) System
Additional trenching will be required to connect the existing, currently exposed conduit to the clubhouse disconnect box. This work is scheduled to begin Monday.
Solar panels are expected to be delivered on Tuesday, with installation anticipated to be completed by the end of the week.
Please note that several parking spaces have been temporarily blocked off to accommodate the solar panel delivery. In addition, Evans will be delivering the remaining roof tiles needed for project completion, and these materials will also be stored in the designated parking areas.
 
Pool Roof Repair
Final shoring has been completed. McCabe Brothers is scheduled to return next week to begin selective demolition of the roof structure. This will allow engineers to fully assess the damage and determine the scope of necessary repairs.
Following that assessment, repair drawings will be prepared and the bidding process for the repair work will begin.
 
We appreciate your patience and cooperation as these important improvements continue. Additional updates will be shared as progress continues.
 
Sincerely,
Ric Gonzalez
Project Coordinator

subject: Scam & Fraud Alerts Available to All Avalon Residents
html:
From: "Steve Slade - Avalon COBWRA (Replies Not Accepted)"
Date: 2026-04-18
Time: 10-57-01
Subject: Scam & Fraud Alerts Available to All Avalon Residents
Stay Informed. Stay Protected.
 
Scam & Fraud Alerts Available for All Avalon Residents

Scam activity targeting homeowners continues to rise,
especially through phone calls, text messages, email, and
social media. As a member of the Coalition of Boynton West
Residential Associations (COBWRA), Avalon Estates
provides continual Scam & Fraud Alerts to help residents
recognize suspicious activity and avoid becoming victims.

Our alerts include:
  • Common scams circulating in Palm Beach County
  • Tips for spotting fraudulent calls, emails, and texts
  • Warnings about utility, contractor, and “urgent
    payment” scams
  • Steps to take if you believe you’ve been targeted
How to receive alerts: Residents can sign up for weekly
updates directly to their email address by registering with
our COBWRA representative, Steve Slade at sgslade@gmail.com.
 
Prior alerts are posted to the Avalon website and may be
accessed via the following sequence: 
  • Log into the Avalon Estates website with your Username and
    Password,
  • Under the “HOA Information” tab, select “Documents/Minutes”,
  • Under “Groups” select “COBWRA”, after which select the desired
    sub directory,  
  • All current weekly alerts are filed under the “2026” tab.
Staying aware is one of the strongest defenses. A few seconds of
caution can prevent a costly mistake.
